campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public hearing matters and upcoming community events, including the Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: Staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Boogie Festival this weekend
Public hearing on government code section 3502
Housing security issues

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to approve financial and infrastructure reports for the fiscal year, schedule a public hearing on a new ordinance, and explore whether the city should become a charter city. The council also reviewed potential new tax options and revenue estimates to help guide future budget decisions.
keyTopics: Preliminary approval of Engineer's Report for fiscal year operations
Public hearing scheduled on a new city ordinance
Exploring charter city status and next steps
Review of potential new taxes and revenue options

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 16, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met on June 16, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and authorize the Town Manager to sign several contract amendments and new agreements for legal services and software. The council also adopted a resolution, though the specific details of that resolution were cut off in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from June 2 and June 8
Authorized new and amended contracts for legal services
Authorized a five-year software services agreement
Authorized litigation legal services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to discuss several major city matters, including a proposal to designate a historic building at 647 South Sixth Street as a city landmark, a potential change to how the city elects mayors and council members when there's a vacancy, and decisions about advertising at the airport and a long-term energy contract.
keyTopics: Historic landmark designation and Mills Act contract for 647 South Sixth Street property
Ballot measure to allow ranked choice voting for mayor and council member vacancies
Award decision for large format advertising signs at San José airport
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council met to address financial planning for the next fiscal year, approve water management updates, and celebrate community milestones. The council also discussed two pending lawsuits and honored City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to the judiciary.
keyTopics: Budget and spending limits for fiscal year 2026-27
Water shortage plans and 5-year management updates
Two anticipated litigation cases requiring legal attention
Capital improvement projects including public safety building and golf course parking lot repairs
Recognition of National Immigrant Heritage Month and Kiwanis Club centennial

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on June 23, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting at 7 p.m. The closed session portion dealt with confidential matters that are not disclosed to the public.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held before regular council session
Regular council meeting convened at 7 p.m.

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review and update Cupertino's Health and Safety Element, a key part of the city's long-term plan through 2040. The council also recognized the Parks and Recreation Department for its work across multiple divisions and celebrated Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team achievement.
keyTopics: Updating Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040)
Recognition of Parks and Recreation Department divisions and services
Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team achievement
July Parks and Recreation Month celebration

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a City Council meeting on June 30, 2026, conducted both in-person and online via Zoom to allow residents to participate remotely. The city provided multiple ways for the public to submit comments and attend, including video conference and phone options.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format allows in-person and remote participation
Zoom meeting access (ID: 997-0675-9306) and phone dial-in available
Public can submit written comments through eComment system on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to address housing and community development funding plans, review requests from individual council members for new work projects, and make procedural changes to how council vacancies are handled. The meeting also included routine items like approving previous meeting minutes and previewing upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development funding plan
Changes to the process for filling council vacancies
New work requests submitted by individual council members
2026-2027 City Council meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 10, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to handle routine administrative matters and consider a zoning change at 3300 El Camino Real. The main decision involves updating building setback requirements (the distance buildings must be set back from the street) on Hansen Way from their current measurement to a standard 20-foot setback.
keyTopics: Zoning setback change at 3300 El Camino Real on Hansen Way
Council schedule and staff assignments
Approval of April Planning & Transportation Commission meeting minutes
